User.title: User
User.description: Represents a faculty user of The Nicole Interface, linked to an external authentication system. Stores basic profile information necessary for application functionality.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity. This ID is typically provided by the external authentication system (e.g., Firebase Auth UUID).

email.description: The email address of the user, primarily used for identification and internal application purposes.

displayName.description: The user's preferred display name, typically retrieved from the authentication provider.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user's profile was first created in the application.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user's profile information was updated.

Module.title: Module
Module.description: Represents an interactive AI learning module within the structured curriculum. Each module features a video introduction and application opportunities with the AI agent.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Module entity.

title.description: The main title of the learning module, clearly outlining its focus.

description.description: A concise summary of the module's content, learning objectives, and benefits.

videoUrl.description: The URL to the module's high-quality video introduction, typically hosted on a platform like Vimeo.

order.description: The sequential order of this module within the overall curriculum, dictating its position in the learning path.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this module was added to the curriculum.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the module's details were updated.

Output.title: Output
Output.description: Represents a high-leverage output (e.g., strategic memo, grant proposal) generated or refined by a user using the Digital Nicole AI Strategist within a module context. These can be saved as drafts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Output entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who generated or refined this output. (Relationship: User 1:N Output)

moduleId.description: Reference to the Module within which this output was generated. (Relationship: Module 1:N Output)

title.description: A user-defined or AI-suggested title for the generated output, for easy identification.

content.description: The main body of the AI-generated or refined text content.

type.description: The category or format of the output, such as 'Strategic Memo', 'Grant Proposal', 'Lesson Plan', or 'Communication Draft'.

toneSettings.description: A string representing the 'Tone Slider' settings or other tone parameters applied during content generation.

status.description: The current status of the output within the user's workflow (e.g., 'draft', 'final', 'archived').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this output was first created or generated.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time this output was saved, updated, or refined.

UserModuleProgress.title: UserModuleProgress
UserModuleProgress.description: Tracks a specific user's progress through a particular learning module, including completion status and last access time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserModuleProgress entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User whose progress is being tracked. (Relationship: User 1:N UserModuleProgress)

moduleId.description: Reference to the Module for which progress is being tracked. (Relationship: Module 1:N UserModuleProgress)

status.description: The current completion status of the module for the user (e.g., 'notStarted', 'inProgress', 'completed').

lastAccessedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user accessed or actively engaged with this module.

completedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user officially marked or achieved completion of the module.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this progress record was initially created (e.g., when the user first started or was assigned the module).

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time this progress record was updated (e.g., status change, last access time update).

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is private, allowing users to manage their own profile data. The document ID matches the Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User, typically matching the Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores definitions for all interactive AI learning modules. This collection is globally readable by all authenticated users. Modules are assumed to be managed by administrators or via background processes.

params.name: moduleId
params.description: The unique identifier for the learning module.

definition.description: Stores high-leverage outputs generated or refined by a user within a module context. Access is private, limited to the owning user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User who owns this output.

params.name: outputId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific output document.

definition.description: Tracks a specific user's progress through a particular learning module. Access is private, limited to the owning user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the User whose module progress is being tracked.

params.name: userModuleProgressId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific user module progress record.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering strictly to the core design principles and mandates. 

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
- For user-specific data like `Output` documents and `UserModuleProgress` records, a hierarchical path structure `/users/{userId}/outputs/{outputId}` and `/users/{userId}/moduleProgress/{userModuleProgressId}` is adopted. 
- A critical aspect for Authorization Independence is that the `userId` field within the `Output` and `UserModuleProgress` documents explicitly stores the `userId` of the owner. This denormalization means that security rules can verify ownership by simply comparing `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path and with `resource.data.userId` in the document itself, without requiring any expensive or complex `get()` operations on parent documents. This ensures atomic operations and simplified rule logic.
- The `Module` collection `/modules/{moduleId}` is designed for global read access by all authenticated users. Since authorization is not user-specific (beyond authentication status), no denormalization for authorization context is required here.

**QAPs (Query, Access, and Permissions) for Secure List Operations:**
- **`/users/{userId}/outputs`**: This subcollection allows users to list *only their own* generated outputs. Security rules can enforce that `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userId}` in the path, making it impossible for a user to query or list outputs belonging to another user. The `userId` field within each `Output` document provides an additional, redundant check for robust security.
- **`/users/{userId}/moduleProgress`**: Similarly, this subcollection ensures users can list *only their own* module progress records. The same rule logic (`request.auth.uid == userId`) applies, guaranteeing secure and efficient listing for individual progress tracking.
- **`/modules`**: All authenticated users can securely list all available learning modules, as these are considered public read-only content. The simple rule `allow read: if request.auth != null;` is sufficient here, enabling QAPs for global module access.

**Structural Segregation & Access Modeling:**
- **Private Data:** User profiles (`/users/{userId}`), outputs (`/users/{userId}/outputs`), and module progress (`/users/{userId}/moduleProgress`) are all segregated under their respective user paths, ensuring a homogenous security posture where only the authenticated user can read/write their own data.
- **Global Read Data:** `Modules` (`/modules/{moduleId}`) are in a top-level collection, clearly delineating them as shared, read-only content for all authenticated users.
- This design minimizes complexity in security rules, moving authorization logic from conditional statements to structural organization.
